posts.title: Axolotls Can Fully Regenerate Lost Limbs, Organs, and Even Brain Parts

pinterest.title: Axolotls Can Fully Regenerate Lost Limbs, Organs, and Even Brain Parts
pinterest.description: The axolotl, a unique type of salamander native to Mexico, possesses an extraordinary ability to regenerate entire lost limbs, spinal cords, jawbones, and even portions of their brains and hearts. Unlike other animals, they heal without scarring and can repeat this process many times. https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/item/axolotl-limb-regeneration-biological-superpower #surfaced #discoveries

posts.title: Ancient Solar Storms Frozen in Antarctic Ice

pinterest.title: Ancient Solar Storms Trapped in Antarctic Ice
pinterest.description: Digging into the past! Scientists found evidence of colossal solar storms, thousands of years old, locked away in Antarctic ice. These weren't your average flares; they were super-storms that dwarf modern events. A cosmic time capsule showing the Sun's wilder side. Could this happen again? #Science #Space #SolarStorms #AntarcticIce

bluesky.text: Imagine solar storms so massive they left their mark in ancient Antarctic ice. Scientists are uncovering evidence of these extreme events from thousands of years ago. A stark reminder of the Sun's power and a warning for our tech-dependent world. What might a modern storm do? 🤔 https://surfaced-x.pages.pages.dev/item/antarctic-ice-reveals-ancient-solar-storm

twitter.text: Ancient solar storms, bigger than anything we've seen, are frozen in Antarctic ice! 🤯 Scientists found evidence of these 'super-storms' from millennia ago. A look at the Sun's violent past and a potential threat to our tech today. How safe are we? https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/item/antarctic-ice-reveals-ancient-solar-storm
